architecture.summary: An interactive LLM agent plans actions by solving a POMDP at each time step for object assembly and troubleshooting tasks. A hybrid-reasoning framework combines confidence scores from the LLM agent's output with a human causal model to select the most appropriate action. The system reads LLM text outputs and a symbolic causal model, then integrates both via a confidence-scoring mechanism to produce final action decisions.

biblio.title: Augmenting large language models with psychologically grounded models of causal reasoning for planning under uncertainty

claims.dataset: object assembly and troubleshooting tasks modeled as POMDPs
claims.statement: augmenting the baseline LLM planner with a human causal model significantly improves task planning reward across three different state-of-the-art LLMs
human causal models can be strategically integrated with Large Language Models to improve planning outcomes under uncertainty for object assembly and troubleshooting tasks
the hybrid-reasoning framework uses confidence scores in both the LLM agent's output and a human causal model to make a final decision on the most appropriate action
